title: 할 때만큼은 그 누구보다 최선을 다하긴 해요 …
hook: 할 때만큼은 그 누구보다 최선을 다하긴 해요 …
caption:
아이러브 스쿼트요…? 🫠

좋아하진 않는데 해야 하니까…
할 때만큼은 그 누구보다 최선을 다하긴 해요 …

근데 물통 들고 워킹런지는 진짜 고난이도 도파민이었어요…
개인운동으로 시작했는데 어느새 피티 받은 기분…

열심히 채찍질도 받고,
이제 그다음은 내가 변화하고 성장해야 할 숙제. 💪

힘든데 너무 재밌어요…
예… 이만. 🫡

@2hmm__official

title: If you’ve been lifting consistently for 1–3 years and feel like your physique hasn’t changed much recently, this is probably the shift you need to make.
hook: If you’ve been lifting consistently for 1–3 years and feel like your physique hasn’t changed much recently, this is probably the shift you need to make.
caption:
If you’ve been lifting consistently for 1–3 years and feel like your physique hasn’t changed much recently, this is probably the shift you need to make.

The beginner phase is incredibly exciting…and forgiving. Your body responds to almost any well-structured training program. As you become more experienced, simply working hard isn’t enough - the QUALITY of the stimulus becomes the limiting factor.

Instead of asking, “How hard was that workout?” start asking, “How much tension did I create in the target muscle?” Those aren’t always the same thing!!

Progressive overload isn’t just adding load

title: It’s pretty cool what happens when you stop letting the scale define your progress. 💗
hook: It’s pretty cool what happens when you stop letting the scale define your progress. 💗
caption:
It’s pretty cool what happens when you stop letting the scale define your progress. 💗

7 months ago, I would’ve looked at these photos and only seen one thing…
+8.6 lbs.

Now I see…
↪️ Stronger shoulders & legs.
↪️ More muscle.
↪️ Better lifts.
↪️ More confidence.
↪️ A healthier relationship with food.

The girl on the left weighed 137.8 lbs.
The girl on the right weighs 146.4 lbs.

For years, I believed being lighter automatically meant I was healthier, happier, and closer to my dream physique. I know so many of you can relate. 🙋🏼‍♀️

But here’s what I’ve learned…

Sometimes your dream physiq

title: Glute activator (sub any glute extension) — 2x10 (1.5 reps) → 2x8-10 heavy
hook: Glute activator (sub any glute extension) — 2x10 (1.5 reps) → 2x8-10 heavy
caption:
LEG DAY

Adductors — 4x15-20

Glute activator (sub any glute extension) — 2x10 (1.5 reps) → 2x8-10 heavy

Standing single leg hamstring curl — 4x12-15

Leg extension — 4x15-20, drop set last round

Drop squat machine — 5 sets, 3 heavy working sets x10-12

Seated leg press — 4x20-25, high rep burnout

Lying hamstring curl — 4x12-15, slow negatives
No shortcuts. Chase the pump, control the tempo, earn the growth.
#LegDay #BodybuildingMotivation #HypertrophyTraining #FitnessCoach #OffSeasonGains
@dfyne.official  code-calfit

title: Want your body to change? Then give it a reason to.
hook: Want your body to change? Then give it a reason to.
caption:
Want your body to change? Then give it a reason to.

One of the biggest mistakes I see is clients doing the exact same workout, with the exact same weights, for months or even YEARS.

Then wondering why nothing changes.

Here's the thing...Your body adapts. If your workouts never become more challenging, your body has no reason to build more muscle.

That's why I encourage clients to track their workouts. Not to obsess over numbers, but to look for progress.

Maybe this week you:

🏋️ Lift 5 pounds more.
🏋️ Complete 2 extra reps.
🏋️ Improve your form.
🏋️ Recover faster between sets.

Those are

title: Before you add another cardio session this week, ask yourself one question:
hook: Before you add another cardio session this week, ask yourself one question:
caption:
Before you add another cardio session this week, ask yourself one question: 

Have I actually maximized my daily movement? Most people haven’t. 

Swipe through to learn why walking may be one of the most underrated fat-loss tools available.

Walking is one of the simplest ways to increase daily calorie expenditure without adding fatigue. 

It’s sustainable, effective, and something almost everyone can do consistently.

More cardio isn’t always the answer. 

Daily movement can burn meaningful calories while helping you recover better so you can keep lifting hard and progressing over time.

title: 7 years in the gym. Still no abs.
hook: 7 years in the gym. Still no abs.
caption:
7 years in the gym. Still no abs.

That was a guy I trained. Smart dude, engineer brain, logical. Seven plus years of showing up. Skinny fat arms. No size.

He came to me with two guesses. Bad genetics, or he needed to start using certain compounds.

Neither one was the problem.

First 12 weeks I dieted him down 8 to 11 pounds. He saw visible abs for the first time in his life.

Around week 12 I taught him the mind muscle connection. Isolating the muscle and holding the contraction instead of just moving the weight.

His arms were getting maybe six sets across the whole week.

By the end of th

title: There wasn’t one workout.
hook: There wasn’t one workout.
caption:

There wasn’t one meal.

It was just me… showing up. Again. And again. And again. ❤️‍🔥

🏋🏽‍♀️ When I felt motivated.
